criteria.label: Write the Ridge and Lasso objective functions and identify L2 vs L1 terms.
criteria.example: State ||y - Xβ||₂² + λΣβⱼ² for Ridge and ||y - Xβ||₂² + λΣ|βⱼ| for Lasso, labeling each penalty type.

criteria.label: Fit Ridge and Lasso with scikit-learn using standardized features and report nonzero Lasso coefficients.
criteria.example: Build make_pipeline(StandardScaler(), Lasso(alpha=0.1)), fit on a dataset, print sum(coef != 0) and compare to Ridge on the same split.

criteria.label: Select lambda via cross-validation and explain the bias–variance tradeoff in the chosen value.
criteria.example: Run RidgeCV over np.logspace(-2, 2, 30), plot CV MSE vs alpha, pick the minimum, and explain why smaller alpha overfits and larger alpha underfits.

criteria.label: Compare Ridge vs Lasso behavior on correlated predictors and recommend one for a stated goal.
criteria.example: Given two collinear size features and a goal of stable coefficients for audit, argue for Ridge; for a minimal gene panel, argue for Lasso sparsity.
